description: US-07 Portion Allowance and Price (domain) — PortionPolicy and PricePolicy plus the describeAllowance helper. Domain and application layers only — the display stories moved into the US-04 batch, because they render onto the counter screen.

userStories.title: PortionPolicy — portions from counts and configured per-head values
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want the portion allowance derived automatically, so I do no arithmetic at a busy counter. Source: tasks/prd-us-07-portions-and-price.md §US-07.1
acceptanceCriteria: Returns grownUps * portionsPerGrownUp + children * portionsPerChild as an integer
No default values are baked into the module — the configuration is a parameter
Tests cover: single-person household, household with children, zero children, portionsPerChild of 0

userStories.title: PricePolicy — the per-head price derivation in cents
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want the price derived from the price per grown-up and per child, so it never flexes with supply or occasion. Source: tasks/prd-us-07-portions-and-price.md §US-07.2
acceptanceCriteria: priceFor(settings, grownUps, children) in src/domain/policy/settings.ts returns grownUps * pricePerGrownUp + children * pricePerChild as integer cents
Looks up an exact (grownUps, children) row — it never interpolates, extrapolates or falls back to a nearest row
Every household size is priceable — an empty household costs 0 and there is no unpriced-household failure mode
Tests cover: exact match, no match, a valid zero-cent row
The module returns cents only; formatting goes through src/domain/money.ts

userStories.title: describeAllowance — counts, portions and price for a customer at a date
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one application helper that resolves settings, derives counts and returns the allowance, so the counter and record screens share it. Source: tasks/prd-us-07-portions-and-price.md §US-07.3
acceptanceCriteria: The helper returns {grownUps, children, portions, priceCents} for a household at a given date
The price it returns is derived from the settings in force, never from a stored column
Test proves a household evaluated on a date where an older settings version was in force is priced with that older version
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
